title: Running runc inside buildah build fails because /sys/fs/cgroup is empty
body:
**Description**

I need to run runc as a part of a container build process. I am unable to do so because /sys/fs/cgroup appears empty and runc errors with "mkdir /sys/fs/cgroup/cpuset/container-id: no such file or directory".

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. Create Containerfile with following contents:
```containerfile
FROM docker.io/ubuntu:rolling

RUN apt-get update
RUN apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends --no-install-suggests \
        skopeo umoci runc ca-certificates

RUN skopeo copy docker://ubuntu:rolling oci:image:latest
RUN umoci unpack --image image:latest bundle && \
    sed -i 's/"bash"/"bash", "-c", "echo hello from runc"/g' bundle/config.json

RUN ls -la /sys/fs/cgroup
RUN script -e -c "runc run -b bundle container-id"
```
2. Run `buildah build`

**Describe the results you received:**
```
STEP 6/7: RUN ls -la /sys/fs/cgroup
total 0
drwxrwxrwt 2 root   root    40 Nov 18 03:40 .
drwxr-xr-x 8 nobody nogroup  0 Nov 15 18:16 ..
STEP 7/7: RUN script -e -c "runc run -b bundle container-id"
Script started, output log file is 'typescript'.
WARN[0000] unable to get oom kill count                  error="no directory specified for memory.oom_control"
ERRO[0000] container_linux.go:380: starting container process caused: process_linux.go:385: applying cgroup configuration for process caused: mkdir /sys/fs/cgroup/cpuset/container-id: no such file or directory
Script done.
error building at STEP "RUN script -e -c "runc run -b bundle container-id"": error while running runtime: exit status 1
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

I expected runc to run properly and execute "echo hello from run".

**Output of `xbps-query buildah`:**

```
architecture: x86_64
changelog: https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/master/CHANGELOG.md
filename-sha256: 385ba3cbb18c49372931fc3022277b7ab3c0384e5d33d78fb0b3b1ef644afc76
filename-size: 7281KB
homepage: https://github.com/containers/buildah
install-date: 2021-11-16 01:16 +07
installed_size: 22MB
license: Apache-2.0
maintainer: Cameron Nemo <cnemo@tutanota.com>
metafile-sha256: 95f1a8fe92dd6861bf0beb9a9dde90738c48138fae303e61aaa389c9d089ebf0
pkgname: buildah
pkgver: buildah-1.23.1_1
repository: https://alpha.de.repo.voidlinux.org/current
run_depends:
        runc>=0
        containers.image>=0
        glibc>=2.32_1
        libgpgme>=1.12.0_2
        libassuan>=2.0.1_1
        libgpg-error>=1.6_1
        device-mapper>=2.02.110_1
shlib-requires:
        libpthread.so.0
        libgpgme.so.11
        libassuan.so.0
        libgpg-error.so.0
        libdl.so.2
        libdevmapper.so.1.02
        libc.so.6
short_desc: Dockerfile compatible OCI image building tool
source-revisions: buildah:af860afe02
state: installed

```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
Version:         1.23.1
Go Version:      go1.17.2
Image Spec:      1.0.1-dev
Runtime Spec:    1.0.2-dev
CNI Spec:        0.4.0
libcni Version:
image Version:   5.16.0
Git Commit:
Built:           Thu Jan  1 07:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
BuildPlatform:   linux/amd64
```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
Linux RijenkiiBook 5.13.19_1 #1 SMP Sat Sep 18 18:18:26 UTC 2021 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

comments.body:
Does 
buildah build --net=private ...

Fix the issue?
@nalind @flouthoc @Luap99 @mheon Is there any reason to default buildah --net=host?

@rhatdan Well CNI does not work correctly in buildah. I plan to fix this in the future by consolidating podman and buildah networking code into c/common.

I do not see how this issue is related to networking.

Yes it is not related to CNI.  My problem is buildah right now is not following the settings in containers.conf which says to default to --net=private --uts=private.  It looks like this is true for podman build as well.  My question is whether we should fix/change the default in Buildah.  Personally I believe we should.

Just in case, `buildah build --net=private` does not help:
```
STEP 6/7: RUN ls -la /sys/fs/cgroup
total 0
drwxrwxrwt 2 root   root    40 Nov 18 16:12 .
drwxr-xr-x 8 nobody nogroup  0 Nov 15 18:16 ..
STEP 7/7: RUN script -e -c "runc run -b bundle container-id"
Script started, output log file is 'typescript'.
WARN[0000] unable to get oom kill count                  error="no directory specified for memory.oom_control"
ERRO[0000] container_linux.go:380: starting container process caused: process_linux.go:385: applying cgroup configuration for process caused: mkdir /sys/fs/cgroup/cpuset/container-id: no such file or directory
Script done.
error building at STEP "RUN script -e -c "runc run -b bundle container-id"": error while running runtime: exit status 1
ERRO[0044] exit status 1
```
I think this is more of a `container-in-container` issue rather than `networking` issue but can tell once i try it out.
No @flouthoc I think  the issue is we are always running builds within the host network namespace, The PR I opened makes Buildah more closely match Podman and follow containers.conf.
